description: Round-2 rebaseline of the promoted dispatch on ion-b200 (same merged sources as repo commit 20c1ba675; no code change). Establishes current truth before the large-S tile reopen: fresh TVM_FFI_CACHE_DIR JIT rebuild (both v2 .so rebuilt), full-grid correctness 237/237 with KDA_REQUIRE_CUDA=1, and six-shape interleaved A/B vs the installed sglang baseline. Baseline drift check: norm.py + rmsnorm_onepass.py byte-identical 0b65588c1..edb1b3f8f (md5 c3f6955e/4c034f80), working tree clean for both. NOTE absolute latencies are inflated vs round-1 rows (host under load: GPUs 2-7 at 53-97%; selected GPU 1 idle 0%/0MiB before+after) - interleaved ratios remain valid, absolute us NOT comparable across rounds. NOTE source_hash 1f4eca379ebcfe4a (merged state) differs from cand-0006-final's 5664640bc35ca28b: cand-0006 was measured pre-merge; PR #23 review edits changed the files before merge. This entry re-baselines the merged state, closing that lineage gap. LN-gate evidence: helios LN candidate 74.38us kernel-event = ~4.76 TB/s = ~59% of ~8 TB/s peak (round-1: 66.08us = ~67%); achieved BW is LOWER under load with no new exploitable headroom signal -> LN gate tentatively CLOSED pending the round-2 design review.

sources_consulted: KernelWiki: query.py 'one pass rmsnorm tiled per head' + --tag rms-norm --architecture sm100 (no new tiled-RMS prior art; fusion PRs noted, out of contract)

description: Tile reopen iteration 1: RmsNormTiledKernel<128,16,bf16> (two 16-lane row segments per warp, register-direct, one CTA per 16-row tile, no smem/cp.async). Correct (35/35 incl. tails + NaN/Inf parity vs pinned baseline) but BEHIND: 0.747/0.762x wall, 0.763/0.771x kernel-event vs pinned Triton on S=648720/650040 (bootstrap CI95 entirely below 1.0). Cause (consistent with prior NCU + design review): 40.5k tiny CTAs each doing one 16B load/lane with no memory-level parallelism - CTA launch/drain dominates and a single dependent load chain per thread cannot hide latency.

evidence.correctness: tests/test_tiled_rms.py 35/35 (R in {16,32} x scheduling in {0,1} x 8 row counts + NaN/Inf + rejection)

sources_consulted: design review (.humanize/skill/2026-06-04_19-07-20): predicted plain-tile risk; ordering register-direct first

description: Tile reopen iteration 2: same R=16 kernel under the persistent occupancy-derived whole-wave grid (grid-stride over tiles). Large gain vs iteration 1 (launch/drain amortized) but still marginally BEHIND the pinned Triton baseline: 0.960/0.967x wall, 0.989/0.988x kernel-event (CI95 below 1.0). One row-pair per warp still leaves a single load in flight per thread between reductions.

Tile reopen iteration 3 (WINNER): R=32 persistent - each warp owns two row-pairs and issues BOTH 16B pair loads before either reduction (memory-level parallelism) inside the persistent grid-stride loop. BEATS the pinned Triton baseline on both huge shapes, both metric kinds, in two independent runs with paired-bootstrap CI95 lower bounds clearing the +2% margin: run1 1.105/1.110x wall + 1.151/1.161x kernel-event (CI-lo 1.0994/1.1057/1.1468/1.1518); rep2 1.100/1.099x wall + 1.141/1.142x kernel-event (CI-lo >= 1.0945). Tiled ~72.4us at S=650040 = ~4.60 TB/s effective (332.8 MB) vs pinned Triton ~83.5-84.7us = ~3.96-4.0 TB/s, under loaded-host conditions (other GPUs busy; selected GPU idle; rep2 saw transient 1180MiB foreign memory at the before-check that cleared - ratios consistent across runs). Matches the recorded re-open condition: tile-based multi-row-per-CTA kernel that beats the baseline. Pending: NCU cross-check (profile/tile_r32_r2/), dispatcher routing + bucket-protection re-verification, then production-path benchmark.

description: Round-2 in-SGLang export refresh (the shipping arbiter). Isolated worktree off edb1b3f8f (PYTHONPATH shadowing, shared checkout untouched, torn down after): round-2 .cuh placed under jit_kernel/csrc/diffusion/, in-tree driver cuda_norm_infer.py (predicates incl. 16B tiled alignment gate + SGLANG_DIFFUSION_NORM_CUDA kill switch), norm.py public-fn insertion, rmsnorm_onepass.py CUSTOM-OP-BODY insertion (RMS registration preserved for every shape on both sides); norm_infer is PUBLIC-FUNCTION symmetric - its baseline hot path carries no custom-op registration (diffusion_layer_norm_fwd_impl_cuda belongs to the separate _layer_norm_fwd helper, not this entry point), so both A/B sides run the same plain public function. Oracle 288/288. Output parity 6/6. SYMMETRIC shipping A/B (identical public callables; RMS registered-op-body symmetric, LN public-function symmetric; device path toggled per iteration): helios 1.2102/1.2397x, rms1320 1.6674/1.6809x, rms4096 1.6467/1.6755x, rms16384 1.6664/1.6810x, rms648720 1.1020/1.1176x, rms650040 1.0950/1.1198x, GEOMEAN 1.3724x wall / 1.3942x kernel-event. Fallback signatures served. Device-vs-host: host layer identical on both sides by construction -> deltas are pure device; consistent with the pinned-lane device-only A/B and the separately quantified custom-op tax. SUPERSEDED BY cand-0014-arbiter-rerun-v4: the tiled kernel's shuffle-mask tail fix (v4) changed the shipped .cuh after this run, so the arbiter was re-executed with the fixed kernel.
command: PYTHONPATH=$WT/python pytest test_qwen_image_modulation.py -q && PYTHONPATH=$WT/python python export/run_export_validation.py

description: Review-driven correctness hardening (v4): the tiled kernel's segmented reduction now uses half-warp shuffle masks (0x0000ffff / 0xffff0000) instead of 0xffffffff - the full-warp mask was undefined behavior whenever an odd row tail left one half-warp inactive (row validity is uniform within a 16-lane half, so the half mask is always exact). Production shapes are even and unaffected; the kernel contract now holds for any S. Odd-tail rows (1,7,15,17,31,33) added to the tile suite: 60/60; full dispatcher regression 297/297 (KDA_REQUIRE_CUDA=1, full grid). Large-S A/B re-confirmation vs pinned baseline (same idle GPU 1): S648720 1.0906x wall / 1.1253x event, S650040 1.0960x / 1.1292x, paired-bootstrap CI95 lower bounds 1.0861-1.1337 (gate 1.02) -> the promote decision stands with the fixed kernel. Production six-shape re-run (cand-0013-dispatch-v4): helios 1.200/1.238, S648720 1.071/1.094, S650040 1.088/1.097 (see csv), small/mid 1.64-1.70, GEOMEAN 1.3580x wall / 1.3892x kernel-event - mask fix is performance-neutral on production shapes.

description: In-SGLang arbiter re-run with the v4 (mask-fixed) .cuh - supersedes cand-0012. Same isolation (fresh worktree off edb1b3f8f, PYTHONPATH shadowing, torn down after; shared checkout clean). SYMMETRY CLARIFIED per the round-0 review: triton_one_pass_rms_norm is custom-op-BODY symmetric (the CUDA branch sits inside the registered _triton_one_pass_rms_norm_cuda body, registration exercised identically on both sides for every shape); norm_infer is public-FUNCTION symmetric - the wrapped public entry carries NO custom-op registration on its hot path (diffusion_layer_norm_fwd_impl_cuda belongs to the separate _layer_norm_fwd helper, never called by this entry point; pinned-lane LN host-layer delta ~1.00x confirms empirically). Results: oracle 288/288; output parity 6/6; shipping A/B helios 1.2146/1.2479x, S648720 1.0913/1.1082x, S650040 1.0881/1.1061x, small/mid 1.66-1.69x, GEOMEAN 1.3722x wall / 1.3946x kernel-event; fallback signatures served (fp16 LN, D=256 RMS, rank-3 RMS).
